title: thomsonaria ochracea
parts: Thomsonaria ochracea has many medicinal parts, such as roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in various traditional remedies. The roots of Thomsonaria ochracea contain compounds like flavonoids and glycosides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of this plant are rich in saponins and terpenoids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The flowers of Thomsonaria ochracea contain anthocyanins and carotenoids, which are responsible for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Thomsonaria ochracea can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause redness, itching, and blistering on the skin. Ingesting parts of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, potentially resulting in dehydration if not treated promptly. Improper handling of the plant can also cause respiratory problems, such as coughing and shortness of breath, in individuals with pre-existing conditions.
